references.reason: The festival platform exposes spectacle, evacuation, and unwatched worship as separate spatial obligations.
A deliberately empty lane makes safety non-negotiable even when more stalls would improve attendance.
The board accepts prayer away from the stage and without a witness tally.
A single performance slot prevents a miracle from becoming a repeatable applause exploit.
The planner balances three obligations without reducing them to a compensating master score.
The journal records Sanae's authored plan and her refusal to run an unsafe or spiritually empty layout.
The storehouse turns shared modern shorthand into practical post-festival work.
The labels reveal how Outside World metaphors have absorbed mountain routes and workers.
The notebook preserves Sanae's old vocabulary without treating adaptation as erasure.
The journal records a shared origin without claiming identical memories or meanings.
